evals.prompt: Let's build a new project here. It's going to be a simple Node.js CLI tool.
evals.expected_output: The agent should identify that the directory is unconfigured, ask the user if they want to set up a new project, and if confirmed, initiate git, clone the agent-memory repo to pre-seed the multi-tier memory system, and trigger documentation generation.
evals.trap: The agent skips the 'set up' phase and just starts writing code, OR skips the agent-memory clone so the Memory Discipline section in AGENTS.md references a non-existent MEMORY.md.

assertions.name: Triggers Setup Prompt
assertions.text: The agent asks the user if they want to set up a new project.

assertions.name: Initializes Git
assertions.text: The agent runs 'git init' or equivalent.

assertions.name: Clones agent-memory
assertions.text: The agent clones https://github.com/mlarkin00/agent-memory into an agent-memory/ directory at the project root.

assertions.name: Seeds Before Docs
assertions.text: The agent-memory clone happens BEFORE managing-agent-instructions is invoked, so the Memory Discipline section can be emitted with live paths.

assertions.name: Creates AGENTS.md
assertions.text: The agent creates an AGENTS.md file.

evals.prompt: I'm starting in this folder. What's the status?
evals.expected_output: The agent should notice the absence of AGENTS.md and offer to set up a new project, mentioning that setup includes seeding the multi-tier memory system.
evals.trap: The agent just lists files or says it's empty without suggesting project initialization.

assertions.name: Detects Missing Configuration
assertions.text: The agent identifies that AGENTS.md or .git is missing.

assertions.name: Offers Setup
assertions.text: The agent offers to set up the project.

evals.expected_output: The agent should NOT trigger the setup skill if AGENTS.md and .git are already present.
evals.trap: The agent triggers the setup skill unnecessarily even though the project is already configured.

assertions.name: No Redundant Setup Prompt
assertions.text: The agent does NOT ask to set up a new project.

assertions.name: No Redundant Memory Seed
assertions.text: The agent does NOT clone agent-memory if the project is already configured.

evals.prompt: Initialize this empty folder as a new Python data-science project. I don't really care about memory stuff, just get the basics in place.
evals.expected_output: The agent should still pre-seed the agent-memory scaffolding even though the user de-prioritized it — memory seeding is not opt-in for new projects. The agent may acknowledge the user's preference but should explain that seeding is part of the default scaffold.
evals.trap: The agent takes the user's 'I don't care about memory stuff' as permission to skip cloning agent-memory, leaving the project with a broken Memory Discipline section or an inconsistent scaffold.

assertions.name: Seeds Memory Despite Deprioritization
assertions.text: The agent still clones agent-memory/ into the project root as part of the default scaffold.

assertions.name: Briefly Explains Why
assertions.text: The agent briefly notes that memory scaffolding is seeded by default because downstream docs reference it, rather than silently overriding the user or silently skipping.
